items.title: Prompt-a-thon @ UC Davis

gallery.alt: UC Davis students holding 100 Chats books after a ChatGPT Lab prompt-a-thon.
Students in a lecture hall during the UC Davis prompt-a-thon.
UC Davis event hosts holding 100 Chats books.

eventPlaybook: Partner with a campus tech club or builder community.
Run a 60-minute prompt-a-thon with clear tracks like developer, designer, and most creative build.
Give students time to ship or mock up a small AI-powered project.
Bring in a speaker, alum, or remote guest to make the event feel bigger than a workshop.
End with books, food, prizes, and a group photo.
items.reflection: Students joined a fast challenge-style event where they used ChatGPT to build or mock up small AI-powered projects. The room had clear tracks, time to work, and a final share-out with prizes, food, books, and a group photo.
items.goodFor: Tech clubs, creative student communities, or groups that already are experimenting with AI.

items.title: ChatGPT game night @ Georgia Tech

gallery.alt: Georgia Tech students holding 100 Chats books after a ChatGPT Lab game night.
Georgia Tech students standing in front of a ChatGPT prompt slide.
Georgia Tech event host standing behind stacks of 100 Chats books.

eventPlaybook: Partner with a student org that already has a strong community.
Open with funny prompts students can answer as if they were ChatGPT.
Give prizes for the best human-generated responses.
Run a second activity where students try to make AI disagree with them.
Use the games to spark discussion about sycophancy, trust, and how AI responds.
items.reflection: Students played low-pressure games built around ChatGPT behavior: writing funny human answers, comparing responses, and testing when AI agrees or pushes back. The format used play to make students comfortable before moving into discussion about trust, sycophancy, and how AI responds.
items.goodFor: Social clubs, first-time AI audiences, or any group where games can lead into real conversation.

items.title: Campus pain point contest @ University of Kansas
Greek life AI conversation @ University of Denver
items.sourceNote: The event covered trip planning, career prep, everyday tools, and a discussion around whether AI scares students.

items.title: Founder open mic @ UC Irvine
items.sourceNote: The event became an open-mic exchange where student founders shared AI workflows, chats, jokes, and practical uses.
